document.title: Risk Management Report For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk analysis, risk evaluation, risk control, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production information for the medical imaging acquisition device.
keyPoints: The report identifies the device as a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector for models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
Related legal requirements include Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC and Medical Device Act requirements.
Related standard requirements include EN 60601-1, EN 60601-1-2, and EN ISO 14971:2012.
Initial identified risks include electrical leakage/current risks, incorrect measurement, erroneous data transfer, loss or deterioration of function, use errors, and information hazards.
After risk control measures, all nine residual risk items are stated to be acceptable.

evidence.claim: The report applies to Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Report For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

evidence.claim: The risk management report was prepared, reviewed, and approved on MAR/26/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Lee, Dong wook MAR/26/2020 Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin MAR/26/2020 Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu MAR/26/2020

evidence.claim: The document revision history lists Rev.0 on March/15/2019 and Rev.1 on MAR/26/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: 0 March/15/2019 Establishment 1 MAR/26/2020 Correction of audit Points

evidence.claim: The report identifies Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC as a legal requirement.
evidence.evidenceText: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC of 5 September 2007 concerning medical devices.

evidence.claim: The report identifies Medical Device Act, Enforcement Regulations of the Medical Device Act, and Standards for Manufacture and Quality Management of Medical Device as legal requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical Device Act, Enforcement Regulations of the Medical Device Act Standards for Manufacture and Quality Management of Medical Device

evidence.claim: The report identifies EN 60601-1, EN 60601-1-2, and EN ISO 14971:2012 as standard requirements.
The intended use role is diagnosis, prevention, monitoring, treatment or alleviation of disease.
evidence.evidenceText: It is the medical device's role relative to - Diagnosis, prevention, monitoring, treatment or alleviation of disease

evidence.claim: The device is not intended to sustain or support life.
evidence.evidenceText: This medical device is not sustain or support life.

evidence.claim: The device is not implanted and is not used on the skin surface of a person's body part.
evidence.evidenceText: It is not the implanted medical device. It is not used on the skin surface of a person’s body part.

evidence.claim: The report states the medical device does not contain software.
evidence.evidenceText: C.2.19 Does the medical device contain software? N/A This medical device does not contain software.

evidence.claim: Risk evaluation criteria classify risk levels 1 to 6 as broadly acceptable and 8 to 25 as not acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Green zone(1~6, Acc, Broadly Acceptable Zone): Risks are acceptable. Red zone(8~25, N/Acc, Not Acceptable Zone)

evidence.claim: Initial unacceptable risks include incorrect measurement, erroneous data transfer, and loss or deterioration of function with risk score 8.
evidence.evidenceText: R04 Incorrect measurement ... 4 2 8 Unacceptable R05 Erroneous data transfer ... 4 2 8 Unacceptable R06 Loss or deterioration of function ... 4 2 8 Unacceptable

evidence.claim: Risk control options are listed in priority order as inherent safety by design, protective measures, and information for safety.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer shall use one or more of the following risk control options in the priority order listed: a) Inherent safety by design ... b) Protective measures ... c) Information for safety

evidence.claim: After risk control, all nine residual risk items are acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: All of nine (9) items have been controlled as Green zone(Broadly Acceptable Zone) that risks are acceptable.

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks are considered acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Overall residual risks are minimized, therefore, it is considered to be acceptable.

evidence.claim: Post-production information is collected through customer satisfaction surveys, internal audit, internet product monitoring, similar-product information, and other information gathering.
evidence.evidenceText: Information on post-production of this product is collected through various channels as below. (1) Research for customer satisfaction (Survey) ... (2) Internal audit ... (3) Product monitoring through internet (4) Information gathering on other companies’ similar products

evidence.claim: The report references an attached IEC60601-1 FMEA risk table file ARMR-FMEA-0-001.
evidence.evidenceText: 10. FMEA (IEC60601-1 Risk Table) See attached file: ARMR-FMEA-0-001

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text cites CE/MDD, Medical Device Act, MFDS, and international requirements; FDA is not supported by the provided source text.
Authority or jurisdiction is partly ambiguous because 'Medical Device Act' is cited without country in the legal requirements section.
The document contains internal count inconsistencies: it lists R01-R12 hazards, states five hazards are derived, evaluates nine listed residual risk items, and says risk factors of 5 Item.
OCR/text quality issues are present, including typographical errors such as 'Risk Application of Risk management', 'possibility of occurrence lever', and 'Not Acceptable Zoon'.
